title: Podman ignores default_network settings on macOS
body:
### Issue Description

Our corporate network has restriction on IP addresses and so we got assigned with the network `192.168.208.0/22` for the local container networks. My understanding is that I need to configure this behaviour in `~/.config/containers/containers.conf`. This is what the file looks like.

```
 [containers]

 [engine]

 env = [
 ]

 [machine]

 rosetta = false

 [network]

 default_subnet = "192.168.208.0/26"

 default_subnet_pools = [
   {"base" = "192.168.208.0/22", "size" = 26},
 ]

 [secrets]

 [configmaps]
```

I try to configure the `default_subnet` with the first network `192.168.208.0/26` and all other networks from the base `192.168.208.0/22`.

```
$ podman version
Client:        Podman Engine
Version:       5.4.1
API Version:   5.4.1
Go Version:    go1.24.1
Built:         Tue Mar 11 18:22:13 2025
Build Origin:  brew
OS/Arch:       darwin/arm64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.4.1
API Version:  5.4.1
Go Version:   go1.23.7
Git Commit:   b79bc8afe796cba51dd906270a7e1056ccdfcf9e
Built:        Tue Mar 11 01:00:00 2025
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

```
$ brew info podman
==> podman: stable 5.4.1 (bottled), HEAD
Tool for managing OCI containers and pods
https://podman.io/
Installed
/opt/homebrew/Cellar/podman/5.4.1 (201 files, 80.3MB) *
  Poured from bottle using the formulae.brew.sh API on 2025-03-12 at 13:47:44
From: https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/HEAD/Formula/p/podman.rb
License: Apache-2.0 AND GPL-3.0-or-later
==> Dependencies
Build: go ✔, go-md2man ✘, make ✔
==> Requirements
Required: macOS >= 13 (or Linux) ✔
==> Options
--HEAD
        Install HEAD version
==> Caveats
In order to run containers locally, podman depends on a Linux kernel.
One can be started manually using `podman machine` from this package.
To start a podman VM automatically at login, also install the cask
"podman-desktop".

zsh completions have been installed to:
  /opt/homebrew/share/zsh/site-functions
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Use configuration I posted.
2. Init and start a machine
```
$ podman machine init
Looking up Podman Machine image at quay.io/podman/machine-os:5.4 to create VM
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 7271b0079ab3 done   |
Copying config 44136fa355 done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
7271b0079ab3c45a82927c6f26ac407ffc1c8bd63b2535bea2fd8fc26f08c6fd
Extracting compressed file: podman-machine-default-arm64.raw: done
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:

        podman machine start

$ podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

        podman machine set --rootful

API forwarding listening on: /var/folders/g0/9p2__phs7ps8_y35llbbt5b80000gn/T/podman/podman-machine-default-api.sock

The system helper service is not installed; the default Docker API socket
address can't be used by podman. If you would like to install it, run the following commands:

        sudo /opt/homebrew/Cellar/podman/5.4.1/bin/podman-mac-helper install
        podman machine stop; podman machine start

You can still connect Docker API clients by setting DOCKER_HOST using the
following command in your terminal session:

        export DOCKER_HOST='unix:///var/folders/g0/9p2__phs7ps8_y35llbbt5b80000gn/T/podman/podman-machine-default-api.sock'

Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
```
3. Check default network
```
$ podman network inspect podman
[
     {
          "name": "podman",
          "id": "2f259bab93aaaaa2542ba43ef33eb990d0999ee1b9924b557b7be53c0b7a1bb9",
          "driver": "bridge",
          "network_interface": "podman0",
          "created": "2025-04-02T13:46:29.609263226+02:00",
          "subnets": [
               {
                    "subnet": "10.88.0.0/16",
                    "gateway": "10.88.0.1"
               }
          ],
          "ipv6_enabled": false,
          "internal": false,
          "dns_enabled": false,
          "ipam_options": {
               "driver": "host-local"
          },
          "containers": {}
     }
]
```

### Describe the results you received

The network is the default network `10.88.0.0/16` instead of `192.168.208.0/26`.

### Describe the results you expected

The default network is `192.168.208.0/26` as configured.

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Macbook Pro running a Apple M1 Pro with 32GB RAM on macOS 15.4

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
is ~ on your mac or in the machine in this case (it needs to be in the machine `podman machine ssh`).  We have a bit of a mess rn where some things are on the host and others in the machine.

You might also need to tidy up your networks once you make that change so it takes effect.

Thanks for the hint. That solved my problem. I was editing the `~/.config/containers/containers.conf` on my mac, not the one in the machine. After editing add the `[network]` configuration to the machines `~/.config/containers/containers.conf`, it worked instantly. So cool. Thanks a lot.

Not sure if we should add this to the documentation or something?!
